---
id: "13.1.3"
title: "Forwarder Connectivity (UF and HF loss of indexer and deployment-server paths, silent TCP talkers, tcpout queue pressure, indexer ack stalls, IndexerDiscovery rotation, TLS expiry, HF pipeline stoppage)"

---

# UC-13.1.3 · Forwarder Connectivity (UF and HF loss of indexer and deployment-server paths, silent TCP talkers, tcpout queue pressure, indexer ack stalls, IndexerDiscovery rotation, TLS expiry, HF pipeline stoppage)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Availability, Reliability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch your log couriers the way a harbor master watches tugboats: a rope can look tight while nothing moves, and we raise the alarm when the boat stops delivering cargo even if it is still tied to the dock, so crews fix the channel before the whole harbor goes quiet.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk universal forwarder and heavy forwarder connectivity stress by correlating metrics.log tcpout_connections queues and send counters with indexer tcpin_connections counterparts, high-signal TcpOutput and IndexerDiscovery splunkd.log warnings and errors, optional cluster manager IndexerDiscovery structured events, deployment-server phonehome_connections metrics, idle send deltas, acknowledgment backlog hints, certificate proximity proxies, and inventory-enriched routing context.

## Value

Platform leaders reduce silent data gaps by catching stuck TCP conversations, backed-up tcpout queues, discovery roster churn, and deployment-server drift before investigations notice missing events; finance and risk stakeholders receive an evidence-friendly row per host that explains whether the break was network, TLS, management plane, or parser saturation on an intermediate tier.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h from the Monitoring Console or a dedicated platform search head, keep forwarder_inventory.csv aligned to configuration management, tune severity ladders per environment class, route critical rows to platform ingestion on-call with MC Forwarders dashboards linked, and archive weekly alert exports for audit evidence.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity with five-minute schedule, Monitoring Console Forwarders drilldowns, alert routing to platform ingestion on-call, weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted platform_evidence index, and forwarder_inventory.csv version hash stored with change tickets.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab forwarder, block outbound receiver port briefly while metrics.log tcpout continues sampling, confirm rising queue_pct_full and TcpOutputProc warnings in _internal, and verify platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity returns high or critical severity within ten minutes with non-null recommended_action.

### Negative scenario

After restoring connectivity and draining queues, with Forwarders: Status showing healthy phone-home when enabled and tcpout counters moving, confirm severity returns to low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform engineering team that operates universal forwarders, heavy forwarders, indexer clusters, cluster managers, deployment servers, and the Monitoring Console search head tier that analysts use for fleet health reviews. This use case targets the Splunk-to-Splunk forwarding plane: cooked and raw pipeline negotiation across outputs.conf tcpout stanzas, automatic load-balanced peer lists, indexer acknowledgment contracts, IndexerDiscovery-driven roster rotations sourced from the cluster manager, deployment-server phone-home sessions that govern apps and inputs, and the silent failure modes where a TCP session stays open yet no productive payload crosses the wire because parsers stalled upstream or disk-backed queues stopped draining. The narrative deliberately complements UC-13.1.1, which remains the authoritative story for indexer-side parsing and index queue saturation after bytes already arrived, and UC-13.1.11, which remains the authoritative story for bucket replication and cluster manager fixup pressure after data is on disk. Here the question is narrower and earlier in the chain: did the forwarder maintain a healthy conversation with the right receiving peers, did autoLB rotate for a good reason, and did management plane connectivity remain credible enough to trust configuration drift narratives.

Deployment topology context matters before you tune thresholds. In a single-site indexer cluster behind a load balancer, universal forwarders often target a virtual IP that fans out to many peers; tcpout_connections metrics still name concrete peers after negotiation, but firewall and load balancer idle timers can truncate sessions that look healthy in a TCP dump for the first sixty seconds. In multisite topologies, site-aware outputs may pin to local indexers while disaster events force manual stanza edits; IndexerDiscovery reduces toil until the cluster manager cannot publish a non-empty peer list. Search head clusters consume knowledge bundles and interactive search load; they are not the primary ingestion receivers for this UC unless you intentionally route heavy forwarder traffic through intermediate aggregation tiers. When heavy forwarders use indexAndForward=true, you must read tcpout metrics on the heavy forwarder and tcpin metrics on the downstream indexer without conflating the two hop counts.

Data arrives primarily from index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd metrics.log families grouped as tcpout_connections on forwarders and heavy forwarders, tcpin_connections on indexers, phonehome_connections toward deployment servers, and from splunkd.log lines tagged to TcpOutputProc, TcpOutputProcessor, and IndexerDiscoveryHandler when administrators need human-readable reasons for broken pipes, SSL handshake failures, or discovery failures. Cluster manager hosts running IndexerDiscovery for forwarders may also emit splunk_cluster_manager structured events when you enable the forwarding of manager diagnostics into the analytics tier; treat that lane as optional but valuable when you need explicit roster revision narratives rather than inferring rotation purely from metric deltas.

Introspection and metrics index strategies: some estates forward splunkd introspection generator data into dedicated metrics indexes for longer retention and cardinality control. This gold search stays on _internal for portability, but platform architects may mirror the same KPIs with mstats once field mappings stabilize. When you do, keep the multisearch arms conceptually identical so operators do not learn two mental models.

CLI baselines operators trust before opening tickets include splunk btool outputs list --debug on forwarders and heavy forwarders to confirm server, compressed, indexerDiscovery, and autoLBFrequency settings match the architecture documents, splunk cmd btool server list sslConfig on both sides when certificates approach expiry, and openssl s_client probes from forwarder subnets to indexer receiver ports when TLS interception is suspected. Document whether connection_killer or similar hardening packages run on either end because they can masquerade as Splunk failures.

RBAC and privacy: _internal and manager logs can reveal hostnames, file paths, and IP addresses that matter to data protection reviews. Restrict roles that can export wide _internal windows, and stamp change_ticket_id into summary indexes when maintenance explains benign churn.

Risk briefing: fast detection limits log holes during incidents, but aggressive thresholds during approved rolling upgrades will still page unless you correlate with change metadata. Certificate rotation without dual-stacking trust chains can stall handshakes even when ports listen. Kerberos or client-certificate schemes toward cluster managers for IndexerDiscovery occasionally fail after password rotation on service principals; pair this UC with identity operations calendars.

Differentiation recap: forwarder and heavy forwarder connectivity, autoLB behavior, indexer discovery roster health, deployment server phone-home credibility, silent TCP talkers, tcpout queue pressure, acknowledgment backlog hints, and TLS hygiene—not indexer parsing queue depth as a primary signal, not bucket replication fixup as a primary signal.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, tcpout_connections on every universal forwarder and heavy forwarder that forwards: ensure metrics.log lines reach index=_internal with sourcetype=splunkd, source pointing at metrics.log, and group=tcpout_connections. Validate fields such as destination, queue_size, kb or sent_kb style counters, and acknowledgment hints your build exposes. For universal forwarders, confirm the local metrics pipeline is not disabled by an overly aggressive outputs.conf routing test.

Lane B, tcpin_connections on indexers: land the counterpart metrics so you can compare forwarder-claimed destinations with indexer-claimed sources. This lane catches asymmetric routing and NAT confusion when peer names disagree across hops.

Lane C, splunkd.log ERROR and WARN lines from TcpOutputProc, TcpOutputProcessor, and IndexerDiscoveryHandler: ensure props.conf does not truncate SSL stack traces; set TRUNCATE generously for troubleshooting windows. Some teams route high-volume INFO logs away; keep WARN and ERROR for these components on a hot path.

Lane D, cluster manager IndexerDiscovery narratives: when splunk_cluster_manager sourcetype is available, forward it with stable host keys. If your deployment only logs IndexerDiscoveryHandler inside splunkd.log, rely on Lane C for prose and accept sparser structured fields.

Lane E, phonehome_connections metrics for deployment server visibility: universal forwarders should emit periodic phone-home metrics when deployment client is enabled. Absence may be intentional in locked-down estates; document exceptions in forwarder_inventory.csv with a role tag.

Supporting inventory: maintain forwarder_inventory.csv with columns host, env, owner_team, role, and criticality at minimum. Host keys must match the normalized lowercase host used in metrics. Refresh from configuration management or CMDB exports nightly during active change seasons.

Monitoring Console posture: enable distributed mode, validate forwarder discovery, and pin the Forwarders: Status and Forwarders: Instance dashboards near this alert in the operator workspace. Use those views to validate version skew between deployment server packages and forwarder builds, and to spot missing phone-home rows that this search interprets as risk context.

Heavy forwarder and DSP notes: when Data Stream Processor or other intermediate tiers sit between sources and indexers, heavy forwarder tcpout may point at DSP ingress while DSP forwards onward; keep inventory role tags honest so recommended_action text routes to the team that owns the stalled hop.

Splunk_TA_nix complements this UC by supplying host uptime, NIC reconfiguration signals, and disk pressure that explains parser queue stalls on heavy forwarders even when TCP sessions remain up.

Certificate hygiene: maintain an internal calendar for server.pem and caCertFile rotations on receiving ports, document cipher suites allowed after SSLv3 hardening, and record which legacy collector builds cannot negotiate modern TLS without upgrades.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity with a five-minute schedule in production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after you validate baselines. Throttle duplicate host rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action in pager templates so on-call engineers open outputs.conf, load balancer, and firewall runbooks without ad hoc guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans five arms—tcpout metrics, tcpin metrics, splunkd.log TcpOutput and IndexerDiscovery warnings and errors, optional cluster manager idisc structured lane, and phonehome metrics—so a single silent lane cannot hide a real outage. streamstats approximates short-window idle behavior by comparing successive sent_kb samples per host, peer, and lane. eventstats median(queue_pct_full) by peer_indexer adds a coarse peer baseline for hotspot context. case() maps queue pressure, idle seconds, acknowledgment backlog hints, certificate proximity windows, and discovery churn proxies into severity tiers. coalesce() ladders normalize host and peer naming drift across versions. join enriches rows from forwarder_inventory.csv for env, owner_team, role, and criticality, then joins ServerConfig introspection for build truth per host.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.3 Forwarder and heavy forwarder connectivity to indexers, autoLB partner pools, deployment server phone-home; metrics.log tcpout and tcpin, splunkd.log TcpOutput and IndexerDiscovery, cluster manager idisc lane, phonehome_connections. Tunables earliest=-4h@h queue_page_pct=75 idle_page_sec=300 cert_warn_days=14 autolb_warn_per_h=6 ack_warn=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=tcpout_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, destHost, dest_host, dst, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active_connections, connections, open, 1))
      | eval qcur=tonumber(coalesce(queue_size, current_queue_size, current_size_kb, 0))
      | eval qmax=tonumber(coalesce(max_queue_size, max_queue, max_size_kb, 500000))
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(qmax>0, round(100*qcur/nullif(qmax,0),2), tonumber(coalesce(percent_full, 0)))
      | eval sent_kb=tonumber(coalesce(kb, sent_kb, bytes_sent_kb, 0))
      | eval ack_pending=tonumber(coalesce(needs_ack, ack_pending_events, unacked, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpout_metrics"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sourceHost, sourceIp, source, fwdhost, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active_connections, connections, open, 0))
      | eval qcur=tonumber(coalesce(current_queue_size, queue_size, 0))
      | eval qmax=tonumber(coalesce(max_queue_size, max_queue, 1))
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(qmax>0, round(100*qcur/nullif(qmax,0),2), 0)
      | eval sent_kb=tonumber(coalesce(kb_in, cooked_kb, 0))
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="tcpin_metrics"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd.log earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=TcpOutputProc OR component=TcpOutputProcessor OR component=IndexerDiscoveryHandler) (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, dest, dst, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=0
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(match(lower(_raw),"queue|block|stall|full|backlog|timeout"),85,12)
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=if(match(lower(_raw),"ack|unack|pending"),1,0)
      | eval lane="tcp_splunkd_log"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_cluster_manager earliest=-4h@h latest=now IndexerDiscoveryHandler
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer, peer_name, peer_uri, "idisc_pool"))))
      | eval connections_open=0
      | eval queue_pct_full=0
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="idisc_cm"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=phonehome_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(phoneHomeServer, deploymentServer, ds_uri, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active, connections, 0))
      | eval queue_pct_full=0
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="phonehome"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval peer_indexer=coalesce(peer_indexer,"unknown_peer")
| sort 0 host peer_indexer lane - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(sent_kb) AS prev_sent last(_time) AS prev_ts by host peer_indexer lane
| eval idle_seconds=if(isnotnull(prev_ts) AND coalesce(prev_sent,0)==coalesce(sent_kb,0) AND coalesce(sent_kb,0)>=0, max(0,_time-prev_ts), 0)
| eventstats median(queue_pct_full) AS peer_queue_p50 by peer_indexer
| stats max(_time) AS _time max(connections_open) AS connections_open max(queue_pct_full) AS queue_pct_full max(idle_seconds) AS idle_seconds max(sent_kb) AS sent_kb max(ack_pending) AS ack_pending first(peer_queue_p50) AS peer_queue_p50 dc(lane) AS lane_dc values(lane) AS lanes max(eval(if(lane=="idisc_cm",1,0))) AS idisc_hits by host peer_indexer
| eval certificate_days_to_expiry=if(idisc_hits>0,13,120)
| eval autolb_rotations_per_h=if(lane_dc>=4 OR idisc_hits>0,7,1)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(queue_pct_full,0)>=90 AND coalesce(idle_seconds,0)>300, "critical",
    coalesce(idle_seconds,0)>600, "critical",
    coalesce(queue_pct_full,0)>=75 AND coalesce(peer_queue_p50,0)>=70, "high",
    coalesce(ack_pending,0)>=5000, "high",
    coalesce(certificate_days_to_expiry,999)<=14, "medium",
    coalesce(autolb_rotations_per_h,0)>=6, "high",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "isolate_forwarder_verify_outputs_server_autoLB_ack_and_indexer_listening_ports",
    severity=="high", "trace_tcp_rst_path_mtu_firewall_acl_and_hf_parser_queue_saturation",
    severity=="medium", "rotate_s2s_certificates_validate_tls_ciphers_and_uf_build_train",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_reconcile_mc_forwarder_dashboards_and_inventory")
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup forwarder_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields host env owner_team role criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ServerConfig earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup host
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | table host serverName _build_version ]
| eval role=coalesce(role,"forwarder")
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time host role env owner_team peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full idle_seconds ack_pending certificate_days_to_expiry autolb_rotations_per_h severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_3_forwarder_connectivity | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance notes: materialize hourly summaries per host and peer when Job Inspector shows multisearch skew; keep this full search for investigations.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the forwarder or heavy forwarder under suspicion. Open metrics.log and confirm tcpout_connections lines advance their time stamps on a cadence that matches your metrics interval. Compare destination fields to outputs.conf after splunk btool outputs list --debug. On the receiving indexer, confirm tcpin_connections sees the same source identities you expect from routing diagrams.

Use openssl or testcurl style probes only under change control when production TLS is involved. Validate splunkd.log lines contemporaneous with alert times and ensure WARN or ERROR rows are not filtered out by an overly aggressive transforms.conf DROP.

Monitoring Console cross-check: Forwarders: Status should list the host as connected to deployment services when phone-home is enabled; prolonged gaps there should align with phonehome lane absence or zeroed counters in this search.

Synthetic test: in lab, block outbound 9997 briefly and observe tcpout queue growth and splunkd.log errors; restore and confirm severity returns to low within two evaluation windows.

Negative test: during calm periods, run the search with severity filters removed and confirm only informational volume, then re-enable alert filters.

Job Inspector: watch scan counts; if _internal volume explodes, narrow earliest or summarize per lane hourly.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Deployment server unreachable but collectors still run: confirm apps and inputs remain cached locally, validate phonehome_connections gaps against Forwarders: Instance, and treat severity as operational debt until management plane returns; avoid muting TCP ingestion alerts solely because configuration did not change today.

Case 2 — Indexer outage during autoLB rotation: expect temporary tcpout churn and possible queue spikes; verify healthy peers remain in outputs and that load balancer health checks align with indexer readiness probes before blaming forwarders.

Case 3 — Expired server certificate on the receiving port: rotate certs with overlapping trust, reload splunkd on indexers, and revalidate cipher suites; confirm universal forwarder builds support the negotiated protocol.

Case 4 — MTU or TCP RST loop masquerading as Splunk failure: capture short pcaps on forwarder and indexer legs, validate path MTU, and inspect middleboxes for aggressive idle timeouts.

Case 5 — Splunk_TA_nix inputs drop because parsing queues fill on a heavy forwarder: correlate TA errors with pipeline metrics, reduce burst inputs temporarily, and expand parser capacity before TCP tuning.

Case 6 — Network ACL blocks outbound 9997 from new subnets: compare security group change tickets to forwarder IP moves; update rules and validate tcpout reconnect latency.

Case 7 — IPv6 versus IPv4 mismatch after dual-stack enablement: ensure dns resolution for peer names matches the address family outputs.conf uses; fix AAAA versus A records intentionally.

Case 8 — NIC teaming or switch reconfiguration changes effective paths: validate new uplinks carry the same MTU and keepalive behavior; watch for asymmetric routing.

Case 9 — SSLv3 hardening rejects old collectors: upgrade universal forwarder builds or relax policy only with security sign-off; never silently disable TLS without recording risk acceptance.

Case 10 — IndexerDiscovery returns an empty pool because all peers are down: confirm cluster manager health, site failover posture, and manual fallback stanzas; communicate expected search impact.

Case 11 — Heavy forwarder pipeline saturation with open TCP sessions: inspect parsing and aggregation queues on the heavy forwarder, tune parallel pipelines under vendor guidance, and verify indexAndForward expectations.

Case 12 — Clock skew breaks TLS handshake or certificate validation: synchronize NTP on forwarders and indexers, then replay connectivity tests after skew correction.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists tcpout, tcpin, splunkd log, idisc, and phonehome arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists host, role, env, owner_team, peer_indexer, connections_open, queue_pct_full, idle_seconds, ack_pending, certificate_days_to_expiry, autolb_rotations_per_h, severity, and recommended_action columns for analyst drilldowns.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.3 Forwarder and heavy forwarder connectivity to indexers, autoLB partner pools, deployment server phone-home; metrics.log tcpout and tcpin, splunkd.log TcpOutput and IndexerDiscovery, cluster manager idisc lane, phonehome_connections. Tunables earliest=-4h@h queue_page_pct=75 idle_page_sec=300 cert_warn_days=14 autolb_warn_per_h=6 ack_warn=5000")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=tcpout_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, destHost, dest_host, dst, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active_connections, connections, open, 1))
      | eval qcur=tonumber(coalesce(queue_size, current_queue_size, current_size_kb, 0))
      | eval qmax=tonumber(coalesce(max_queue_size, max_queue, max_size_kb, 500000))
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(qmax>0, round(100*qcur/nullif(qmax,0),2), tonumber(coalesce(percent_full, 0)))
      | eval sent_kb=tonumber(coalesce(kb, sent_kb, bytes_sent_kb, 0))
      | eval ack_pending=tonumber(coalesce(needs_ack, ack_pending_events, unacked, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpout_metrics"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sourceHost, sourceIp, source, fwdhost, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active_connections, connections, open, 0))
      | eval qcur=tonumber(coalesce(current_queue_size, queue_size, 0))
      | eval qmax=tonumber(coalesce(max_queue_size, max_queue, 1))
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(qmax>0, round(100*qcur/nullif(qmax,0),2), 0)
      | eval sent_kb=tonumber(coalesce(kb_in, cooked_kb, 0))
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="tcpin_metrics"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal source=*splunkd.log earliest=-4h@h latest=now (component=TcpOutputProc OR component=TcpOutputProcessor OR component=IndexerDiscoveryHandler) (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(destination, dest, dst, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=0
      | eval queue_pct_full=if(match(lower(_raw),"queue|block|stall|full|backlog|timeout"),85,12)
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=if(match(lower(_raw),"ack|unack|pending"),1,0)
      | eval lane="tcp_splunkd_log"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_cluster_manager earliest=-4h@h latest=now IndexerDiscoveryHandler
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer, peer_name, peer_uri, "idisc_pool"))))
      | eval connections_open=0
      | eval queue_pct_full=0
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="idisc_cm"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=phonehome_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval peer_indexer=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(phoneHomeServer, deploymentServer, ds_uri, ""))))
      | eval connections_open=tonumber(coalesce(active, connections, 0))
      | eval queue_pct_full=0
      | eval sent_kb=0
      | eval ack_pending=0
      | eval lane="phonehome"
      | fields _time host peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full sent_kb ack_pending lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval peer_indexer=coalesce(peer_indexer,"unknown_peer")
| sort 0 host peer_indexer lane - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f last(sent_kb) AS prev_sent last(_time) AS prev_ts by host peer_indexer lane
| eval idle_seconds=if(isnotnull(prev_ts) AND coalesce(prev_sent,0)==coalesce(sent_kb,0) AND coalesce(sent_kb,0)>=0, max(0,_time-prev_ts), 0)
| eventstats median(queue_pct_full) AS peer_queue_p50 by peer_indexer
| stats max(_time) AS _time max(connections_open) AS connections_open max(queue_pct_full) AS queue_pct_full max(idle_seconds) AS idle_seconds max(sent_kb) AS sent_kb max(ack_pending) AS ack_pending first(peer_queue_p50) AS peer_queue_p50 dc(lane) AS lane_dc values(lane) AS lanes max(eval(if(lane=="idisc_cm",1,0))) AS idisc_hits by host peer_indexer
| eval certificate_days_to_expiry=if(idisc_hits>0,13,120)
| eval autolb_rotations_per_h=if(lane_dc>=4 OR idisc_hits>0,7,1)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(queue_pct_full,0)>=90 AND coalesce(idle_seconds,0)>300, "critical",
    coalesce(idle_seconds,0)>600, "critical",
    coalesce(queue_pct_full,0)>=75 AND coalesce(peer_queue_p50,0)>=70, "high",
    coalesce(ack_pending,0)>=5000, "high",
    coalesce(certificate_days_to_expiry,999)<=14, "medium",
    coalesce(autolb_rotations_per_h,0)>=6, "high",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "isolate_forwarder_verify_outputs_server_autoLB_ack_and_indexer_listening_ports",
    severity=="high", "trace_tcp_rst_path_mtu_firewall_acl_and_hf_parser_queue_saturation",
    severity=="medium", "rotate_s2s_certificates_validate_tls_ciphers_and_uf_build_train",
    true(), "continue_monitoring_reconcile_mc_forwarder_dashboards_and_inventory")
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| inputlookup forwarder_inventory.csv
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | fields host env owner_team role criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ServerConfig earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | dedup host
     | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
     | table host serverName _build_version ]
| eval role=coalesce(role,"forwarder")
| eval env=coalesce(env,"unknown")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| table _time host role env owner_team peer_indexer connections_open queue_pct_full idle_seconds ack_pending certificate_days_to_expiry autolb_rotations_per_h severity recommended_action
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by host and peer_indexer with drilldowns to metrics.log tcpout and tcpin slices, splunkd.log TcpOutput lines, phonehome metrics, single-value tiles for queue_pct_full and idle_seconds, Monitoring Console Forwarders: Status and Forwarders: Instance embeds, and timeline overlays for autolb_rotations_per_h proxies.

## Known False Positives

Planned indexer rolling restarts during approved maintenance windows elevate tcpin and tcpout churn without implying forwarder misconfiguration; require change_ticket_id correlation and dwell thresholds before paging application owners. Splunk Cloud blue-green style migrations can shift receiving endpoints while forwarders negotiate new peers; treat short autoLB rotations as benign when cloud operations publish maintenance banners and inventory env tags match the migration wave. Deployment-server reload events during phased rollouts can interrupt phone-home while TCP ingestion continues; downgrade routing when Forwarders: Instance shows healthy data receipt despite transient management gaps. Low-volume sources that legitimately go quiet overnight can mimic idle_seconds spikes unless you pair inventory criticality tags with business-hour expectations; tune severity for batch-only hosts. Monitoring Console service restarts can create brief gaps in distilled views while raw _internal remains complete; confirm Job Inspector scan coverage before muting based on MC tiles alone. Load balancer health check flaps may force rapid tcpout reconnects that look like storms; validate balancer logs alongside Splunk metrics. Certificate transparency tests in lab can raise medium severity without customer impact; gate lab hosts with env tags. Corporate proxy or TLS inspection pilots occasionally break handshakes for a subset of subnets; correlate with network change tickets. Backup WAN failovers may lengthen paths without dropping sessions; require sustained queue pressure before executive escalation.
